1→# Review: deepmind/662.lean
     2→
     3→## 1. Copyright Header
     4→- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
     5→- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
     6→
     7→## 2. Import Statement
     8→- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
     9→- [x] No other imports are present.
    10→
    11→## 3. Module Docstring
    12→- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
    13→- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 662`.
    14→- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/662](https://www.erdosproblems.com/662)`.
    15→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (triangular lattice, close-pair counting, optimality conjecture).
    16→- [x] Optional bibliography: references `[Er97e]` and notes the ambiguity in the original statement.
    17→
    18→## 4. Namespace
    19→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos662 ... end Erdos662`.
    20→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos662`.
    21→- [x] `open Classical` appears **before** the namespace block.
    22→
    23→## 5. Custom Definitions
    24→- [x] `triangularLatticeNeighborCount` has a LaTeX docstring explaining the squared-distance formula in the triangular lattice.
    25→- [x] `closePairCount` has a LaTeX docstring describing the ordered-pair count.
    26→- [x] Both definitions are marked `noncomputable` (they use `Set.ncard` and `Finset.filter`/`.card` over non-decidable predicates).
    27→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    28→- [x] Functions use `lowerCamelCase`: `triangularLatticeNeighborCount`, `closePairCount`.
    29→- N/A: No local notation needed; no custom types/Props defined.
    30→- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional, not required).
    31→
    32→## 6. Theorem Statements
    33→- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
    34→- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_662` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
    35→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 52]`.
    36→- [x] Has a docstring referencing the source: `[Er97e, p.532]`.
    37→
    38→### `answer()` usage
    39→The problem asks "Is it true that …" — this is a **yes/no question (Case 1)**.
    40→- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`.
    41→- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (triangular lattice maximizes close pairs among unit-separated point sets, for sufficiently large `n` depending on `t`).
    42→- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ t`, `∃ N`, `∀ n`, `∀ P`) are **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.
    43→
    44→## 7. Category Attribute
    45→- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
    46→- [x] Value is `research open` — valid.
    47→- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.
    48→
    49→## 8. AMS Attribute
    50→- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS 52]` attribute (not multiple separate `@[AMS]` tags).
    51→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `52`.
    52→- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single entry — trivially satisfied).
    53→- [x] No duplicates.
    54→- [x] `52` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Convex and discrete geometry).
    55→
    56→## 9. Proof Bodies
    57→- [x] Open conjecture uses `by` with `sorry` on the next indented line — acceptable multi-line form per checklist §9.
    58→- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `noncomputable def` declarations.
    59→- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
    60→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    61→
    62→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    63→- N/A: No variants present. The file contains a single main theorem.
    64→
    65→## 11. Code Quality
    66→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    67→- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`ℝ`, `ℤ`, `ℕ`, `∀`, `∃`, `≠`, `≥`, `≤`, `↔`).
    68→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    69→- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
    70→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
    71→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    72→
    73→## 12. Build Verification
    74→- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
    75→- [ ] Custom linter pass — not verified in this review.
    76→
    77→## Summary
    78→
    79→**Result: PASS** (modulo build verification)
    80→
    81→The file is clean and fully conformant. The copyright header, import, module docstring, namespace, definitions, theorem statement, attributes, `answer()` usage, and code quality all meet the checklist requirements. The `answer(sorry) ↔ P` form correctly reflects the yes/no nature of the original problem ("Is it true that …"). Both helper definitions (`triangularLatticeNeighborCount`, `closePairCount`) are well-documented with docstrings, appropriately marked `noncomputable`, and use `lowerCamelCase`. No issues found.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/662.lean

## 1. Copyright Header
- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement
- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring
- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 662`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/662](https://www.erdosproblems.com/662)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (triangular lattice, close-pair counting, optimality conjecture).
- [x] Optional bibliography: references `[Er97e]` and notes the ambiguity in the original statement.

## 4. Namespace
- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos662 ... end Erdos662`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos662`.
- [x] `open Classical` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions
- [x] `triangularLatticeNeighborCount` has a LaTeX docstring explaining the squared-distance formula in the triangular lattice.
- [x] `closePairCount` has a LaTeX docstring describing the ordered-pair count.
- [x] Both definitions are marked `noncomputable` (they use `Set.ncard` and `Finset.filter`/`.card` over non-decidable predicates).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Functions use `lowerCamelCase`: `triangularLatticeNeighborCount`, `closePairCount`.
- N/A: No local notation needed; no custom types/Props defined.
- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional, not required).

## 6. Theorem Statements
- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_662` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 52]`.
- [x] Has a docstring referencing the source: `[Er97e, p.532]`.

### `answer()` usage
The problem asks "Is it true that …" — this is a **yes/no question (Case 1)**.
- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`.
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (triangular lattice maximizes close pairs among unit-separated point sets, for sufficiently large `n` depending on `t`).
- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ t`, `∃ N`, `∀ n`, `∀ P`) are **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute
- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research open` — valid.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute
- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS 52]` attribute (not multiple separate `@[AMS]` tags).
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `52`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single entry — trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicates.
- [x] `52` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Convex and discrete geometry).

## 9. Proof Bodies
- [x] Open conjecture uses `by` with `sorry` on the next indented line — acceptable multi-line form per checklist §9.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `noncomputable def` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
- N/A: No variants present. The file contains a single main theorem.

## 11. Code Quality
- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`ℝ`, `ℤ`, `ℕ`, `∀`, `∃`, `≠`, `≥`, `≤`, `↔`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification
- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linter pass — not verified in this review.

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (modulo build verification)

The file is clean and fully conformant. The copyright header, import, module docstring, namespace, definitions, theorem statement, attributes, `answer()` usage, and code quality all meet the checklist requirements. The `answer(sorry) ↔ P` form correctly reflects the yes/no nature of the original problem ("Is it true that …"). Both helper definitions (`triangularLatticeNeighborCount`, `closePairCount`) are well-documented with docstrings, appropriately marked `noncomputable`, and use `lowerCamelCase`. No issues found.

content.text: The review passed (**Result: PASS**). No changes needed.
